job__vehicle_information.from_: Mike Harrison - Cedar Valley Collision Repair

cut_instructions.please_use_this_area_to_explain_your_cut:
Customer vehicle: 2016 Toyota RAV4 XLE AWD, Blizzard Pearl (white), VIN and tags already documented on our end. Vehicle sustained severe right-rear impact. We need a clean rear body section to correct structural damage.

GENERAL REQUEST
We are looking for a complete rear body clip from a matching or compatible 2013–2018 Toyota RAV4, preferably an XLE or Limited with factory AWD, in white if available (color match is not critical but preferred). Rust-free, no collision damage in the rear structure, and no kinks in the frame rails or roof.

OVERALL CUT PARAMETERS
Please provide a full rear clip including:
- Rear frame rails from just ahead of the rear suspension cradle
- Rear floor pan from just forward of the spare tire well back to the rear bumper mounting area
- Rear body panel (including latch area, bumper reinforcement mounting points, and impact bar mounts)
- Both rear inner quarter structures
- Rear hatch opening structure (full aperture)
- Roof panel section from approximately the B-pillars rearward

SPECIFIC CUT LINES (TOP, SIDES, FLOOR)

1) ROOF CUT:
- Make the primary roof cut vertically through the roof panel approximately 3–4 inches forward of the C-pillars, between the B- and C-pillars.
- Cut straight across from left to right, keeping the inner roof reinforcement and any associated braces intact on the donor clip side.
- Do NOT cut directly through the C-pillars; we want the full C-pillar structure with at least 3–4 inches of roof skin ahead of it for us to work with during the graft.
- Retain the upper portions of the D-pillar (if applicable) and the full hatch opening frame.

2) SIDE / QUARTER PANEL CUTS:
- On both left and right sides, include the full rear quarter panels, inner and outer, up to just ahead of the rear wheel arch.
- Make vertical cuts down the sail panel area about 3–4 inches forward of the rear wheel opening.
- On the lower rocker area, cut approximately 3–4 inches forward of the rear door opening seam so that we have overlap material to work with.
- Please keep the fuel filler pocket and surrounding structure fully intact on the left side, including the inner reinforcement.
- Do not worry about preserving the rear doors or glass; these can be removed prior to the cut.

3) FLOOR / FRAME RAIL CUTS:
- On the rear frame rails, cut just forward of the rear suspension cradle mounts—roughly mid-vehicle behind the rear seats. The exact location can be where the rails are straight and free of factory seams.
- Include the entire spare tire well and rear cargo floor area.
- Include the rear crossmember(s) that tie the frame rails together.
- Make sure the rear bumper reinforcement mounting points and crash bar mounts are intact and undamaged.

4) HATCH OPENING & REAR PANEL:
- We need the complete hatch opening structure, including the upper hinge mounting points, latch striker area, and all surrounding metal.
- Include the entire rear body panel (tail lamp pockets, latch area, and lower valance mounting structure).
- Tail lamps, wiring, and trim can be removed; we mainly need the bare metal shell, but if keeping the tail lamp pockets intact is easier, that is preferred.

PARTS WE DO **NOT** NEED ATTACHED (CAN BE REMOVED TO SAVE SHIPPING WEIGHT):
- Rear suspension components (trailing arms, springs, shocks, etc.)
- Fuel tank and fuel lines
- Exhaust system
- Interior trim panels, carpet, seats, and interior plastics
- Rear bumper cover and foam absorber (metal impact bar can remain attached if it’s easier for you)
- Wiring harnesses (main body harness can be cut; we do not need it complete)
- Glass: rear hatch glass and fixed quarter glass can be removed; we only need the metal structure

CONDITION REQUIREMENTS:
- No prior collision damage, pulling, or kinks in the rear rails, roof, or hatch opening.
- No significant rust, especially in the rear frame rails, spare tire well, and quarter panel lower edges.
- Minor surface rust is acceptable, but no perforation.
- Please avoid vehicles that have been rear-ended; side or front impact donors are okay as long as the rear is clean.

HANDLING & LABELING REQUESTS:
- Please leave at least 2–3 inches of extra material beyond all factory seams whenever possible, so our body techs have room to trim and fit during installation.
- Mark your cut lines with paint or marker before cutting to ensure straight, consistent cuts.
- After cutting, please deburr or smooth any extremely sharp protruding edges that could cause injury during handling.
- If possible, spray a light coat of rust inhibitor or primer on any freshly cut edges before shipping.

SHIPPING & MOUNTING NOTES:
- We would appreciate if the clip can be banded or strapped to a pallet, with the roof side up and the frame rails supported.
- Please avoid bending the hatch opening or roof during loading/unloading.
- If the donor vehicle has a roof rack or antenna base in the area of the roof cut, it’s fine to remove those before cutting.

COLOR PREFERENCE (NOT REQUIRED):
- Preferred color: Blizzard Pearl or any white. However, we will be repainting, so color is not a deal-breaker. Structural integrity is the priority.

IF THERE ARE ANY QUESTIONS:
- If anything about these instructions is unclear or if you have a slightly different cut that works better with your equipment or the donor vehicle’s condition, please call before cutting so we can confirm.
- Contact: Mike Harrison, Cedar Valley Collision Repair, shop line (319) 555-2487, email mike.harrison@cedarvalleycollision.com.

Purpose of this clip: The customer’s 2016 RAV4 sustained heavy right-rear impact that pushed the rear rail, quarter, and hatch opening out of spec. Our frame rack can pull it somewhat, but the metal is stretched and kinked beyond safe repair. We intend to replace the full rear body section from just behind the rear doors back, tying into the existing structure at clean, straight locations. That’s why we’re asking for extra overlap on all cut edges and a complete, unmolested rear structure.
